title: Docker Container Port Mapping Validation Bug Fixer

statement.markdown: Given a string of Docker container port mappings in the format `containerName:port`, validate if the port number is within the allowed range (1024-65535). Return `true` if the port is valid, `false` otherwise. For example, `web:8080` is valid, but `web:123` is not. If there are multiple mappings, they can be separated by commas, e.g., `web:8080, db:54321`.
statement.inputFormat: A string of container port mappings
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the port is valid
statement.notes: Assume the input string is well-formed and does not contain any invalid characters.

examples.explanation: Both ports are within the allowed range
The port 123 is not within the allowed range
The port 65536 is outside the allowed range

hints.text: Split the input string into individual port mappings
